Attribute.Documentation:
we do not load all accounts' entries when retrieving them from DB

adding new entries requires optimistic lock
Attribute.Documentation_plain: we do not load all accounts' entries when retrieving them from DB adding new entries requires optimistic lock
Attribute.Name: newEntries

Class.Documentation:
Represents the accounting classification of an account.

Account types group accounts based on their role in the financial system,

such as assets, liabilities, revenues, expenses, or off-balance tracking.
Class.Documentation_plain: Represents the accounting classification of an account. Account types group accounts based on their role in the financial system, such as assets, liabilities, revenues, expenses, or off-balance tracking.
Class.Name: AccountType

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
Represents accounts that record owned economic resources with possiblePlan productName.

Asset accounts include receivables, cash, and other resources controlled by the entity

expected to bring economic benefit.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Represents accounts that record owned economic resources with possiblePlan productName. Asset accounts include receivables, cash, and other resources controlled by the entity expected to bring economic benefit.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: ASSET

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
Represents off-balance-sheet accounts used for tracking informational, contingent,

or projected values that do not directly affect the financial statements.

These accounts are often used for collateral, guarantees, or estimated figures.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Represents off-balance-sheet accounts used for tracking informational, contingent, or projected values that do not directly affect the financial statements. These accounts are often used for collateral, guarantees, or estimated figures.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: OFF_BALANCE

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
Represents accounts used to record outflows of economic resources,

typically associated with the costs of operations or losses.

Expense accounts reduce the entity's equity and are used in profit & loss reporting.

Examples include operational costs, write-offs, penalties, or losses due to fraud.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Represents accounts used to record outflows of economic resources, typically associated with the costs of operations or losses. Expense accounts reduce the entity's equity and are used in profit & loss reporting. Examples include operational costs, write-offs, penalties, or losses due to fraud.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: EXPENSE

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
Represents accounts that record obligations or debts owed by the entity.

Liability accounts include payables, loans, accrued expenses, and other financial

obligations that the entity must settle in the future.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Represents accounts that record obligations or debts owed by the entity. Liability accounts include payables, loans, accrued expenses, and other financial obligations that the entity must settle in the future.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: LIABILITY

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
Represents accounts used to record inflows of economic resources,

typically associated with sales, services rendered, or other income-generating activities.

Revenue accounts increase the entity's equity and are used in profit & loss reporting.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: Represents accounts used to record inflows of economic resources, typically associated with sales, services rendered, or other income-generating activities. Revenue accounts increase the entity's equity and are used in profit & loss reporting.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: REVENUE

Operation.Documentation: TODO: powino zostaÄ‡ przeniesione do widoku - ta klasa sĹ‚uzy tylko do przechowywania nowych wpisĂłw - nie wszystkich
Operation.Documentation_plain: TODO: powino zostaÄ‡ przeniesione do widoku - ta klasa sĹ‚uzy tylko do przechowywania nowych wpisĂłw - nie wszystkich
Operation.Name: balanceAsOf

Class.Documentation: it is an alternative to searching for entries through accounts
Class.Documentation_plain: it is an alternative to searching for entries through accounts
Class.Name: EntryRepository

Operation.Documentation:
IF posting rules are outside the main accounting model

calculator should return ExecuteTransactionCommand instead
Operation.Documentation_plain: IF posting rules are outside the main accounting model calculator should return ExecuteTransactionCommand instead
Operation.Name: calculate

Class.Documentation:
Facade for PostingRules module.

Note: This facade intentionally exposes domain objects (PostingRule, PostingRuleBuilder)

and interfaces (EligibilityCondition, PostingCalculator) to demonstrate the rich domain model capabilities.

In a production solution, we recommend transitioning to command-based API (CreatePostingRule, UpdatePostingRule, etc.)

to better encapsulate the domain model and provide clearer boundaries.
Class.Documentation_plain:
Facade for PostingRules module.
Note: This facade intentionally exposes domain objects (PostingRule, PostingRuleBuilder) and interfaces (EligibilityCondition, PostingCalculator) to demonstrate the rich domain model capabilities. In a production solution, we recommend transitioning to command-based API (CreatePostingRule, UpdatePostingRule, etc.) to better encapsulate the domain model and provide clearer boundaries.
Class.Name: PostingRulesFacade

Attribute.Name: BALANCING_CONSTRAINT
MIN_2_ENTRIES_CONSTRAINT
MIN_2_ACCOUNTS_INVOLVED_CONSTRAINT

Class.Documentation:
Builder for creating expiration compensation transactions.

<p>

Note: A scheduler service could be implemented to automatically find expired entries

using EntryRepository.findAllMatching() with validity.hasExpired() predicate,

and then call this builder to create compensation transactions via AccountingFacade.
Class.Documentation_plain:
Builder for creating expiration compensation transactions.

Note: A scheduler service could be implemented to automatically find expired entries using EntryRepository.findAllMatching() with validity.hasExpired() predicate, and then call this builder to create compensation transactions via AccountingFacade.
Class.Name: ExpirationCompensationTransactionEntriesBuilder

Operation.Documentation: ========== Account Creation with Initial Balances ==========
Operation.Name: can_create_accounts_with_initial_balances

Operation.Name: should_find_existing_account

Class.Documentation:
Banking - Loan Repayment Breakdown.

Loan repayment split into principal, interest and fees.
Class.Documentation_plain: Banking - Loan Repayment Breakdown. Loan repayment split into principal, interest and fees.
Class.Name: BankingScenarios

Class.Documentation:
Bi-temporal - occurredAt vs appliesAt.

Distinction between event time and application time.
Class.Documentation_plain: Bi-temporal - occurredAt vs appliesAt. Distinction between event time and application time.
Class.Name: BiTemporalScenarios

Class.Documentation:
E-commerce - Customer Refund with VAT.

Customer refund with proper VAT handling.
Class.Documentation_plain: E-commerce - Customer Refund with VAT. Customer refund with proper VAT handling.
Class.Name: EcommerceScenarios

Class.Documentation:
Fleet Card Limit with Validity.

Fleet card limit with time-based validity periods.
Class.Documentation_plain: Fleet Card Limit with Validity. Fleet card limit with time-based validity periods.
Class.Name: FleetCardScenarios

Class.Documentation:
Telco Bonus with Validity.

Telco bonus with time-based validity (e.g., free minutes, GB).
Class.Documentation_plain: Telco Bonus with Validity. Telco bonus with time-based validity (e.g., free minutes, GB).
Class.Name: TelcoBonusScenarios

we do not load all accounts' entries when retrieving them from DB

adding new entries requires optimistic lock
AssociationEnd.Documentation_plain: we do not load all accounts' entries when retrieving them from DB adding new entries requires optimistic lock
AssociationEnd.Name: newEntries
